description: A design works intermittently because an asynchronous clock-domain crossing lacks a synchronizer and goes metastable.
body: Failure mode: a signal crosses between unrelated clock domains through a single flip-flop, so it occasionally samples a transitioning value and propagates a metastable state. Detection signals: rare nondeterministic glitches, failures that change with temperature or voltage, and a CDC report flagging an unsynchronized crossing. Do not dismiss intermittent faults as noise while a bare crossing exists. Safer fallback: insert a multi-flop synchronizer or handshake on the crossing, apply asynchronous constraints, and re-run CDC analysis.
